---
title: "[TBD] Frictions Frequency and Modality vs User Satifaction"
---

This project investigates how the frequency and modality of design frictions in social media feeds affect user satisfaction. We implement a content feed with configurable friction mechanisms — such as mini-games, pause screens, content feedback prompts, and interactive toggles — and compare content dwell time against an infinite-scroll control group.

See the [Project Proposal](about.qmd) for details on the target paper, implementation scope, and evaluation methodology.
